fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: California Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in California — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license california, dental assistant certification california, RDA california, DANB CDA california, dental assistant radiography california

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 25 units of continuing education every two years

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant (RDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in California.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility and Examination Requirements
steps.description: Complete an approved Registered Dental Assisting program or obtain 15 months of satisfactory work experience. You must also complete required courses in the Dental Practice Act, Infection Control, and Basic Life Support (BLS), and pass the California RDA Written Examination, Coronal Polishing, Sealants, and Oral Radiography exams.

steps.title: Create a BreEZe Account
steps.description: If you are a new applicant, register for an account on the BreEZe Online Services portal at www.breeze.ca.gov. This system is used by various California boards for professional licensing.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your BreEZe account, select 'Start a New Application' from the main menu, and choose the 'Registered Dental Assistant (RDA)' application type. Complete all sections of the application form accurately.

steps.title: Complete Live Scan Fingerprinting
steps.description: Download the Live Scan Request form from the Dental Board of California's website. Take the completed form to an authorized Live Scan provider in California for a criminal background check. Ensure the results are sent directly to the Board.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Electronically upload or mail all required documentation to the Dental Board, as specified in your BreEZe application or on the Board's website. This includes official transcripts, course completion certificates, and verification of examination results.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Pay the required application fee and the initial license fee through the BreEZe Online Services portal using a credit card. Your application will not be processed until all fees are received.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the Dental Board of California by registration.

faqs.question: Does California require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, California requires Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in California?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in California.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in California?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in California?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $120. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: California requires RDA registration. DANB CDA holders may apply for RDA without repeating training as of July 2025.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in California
hero.dek: To apply for a Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) license in California, you must submit your application and supporting documentation through the BreEZe Online Services portal after meeting all eligibility and examination requirements.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the BreEZe Online Services portal at www.breeze.ca.gov. Click 'New User? Register Here' to create an account, then log in and select 'Start a New Application' from the dropdown menu to find and begin the Registered Dental Assistant application.
